title: The Seven Principles for Making Marriage Work: A Practical Guide from the Country's Foremost Relationship Expert
subtitle: The most research-grounded book ever written on what makes relationships succeed — based on decades of observational research.
author: John Gottman & Nan Silver
themes: The Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse — criticism, contempt, defensiveness, stonewalling
The magic ratio — 5 positive interactions to 1 negative
The importance of bids for connection and turning toward instead of away
Creating shared meaning and a culture of appreciation
Understanding your partner's inner world through love maps
excerpt: John Gottman is the most famous relationship researcher in the world. His Seattle lab has observed thousands of couples for decades, and he can predict with 91% accuracy which couples will divorce after watching just a few minutes of interaction. This book distills his research into seven principles that distinguish masters of relationships from disasters of relationships — and provides practical exercises for building a stronger, more resilient partnership.
